mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month employment growth (PAYEMS) signals robust labor market momentum and consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through increased corporate earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2021 labor market recovery from COVID-19 shock
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth does not correlate with positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) begins accelerating above trend, equity investors perceive real rates as deeply negative, triggering a flight-to-real-assets that drives S&P 500 outperformance over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2021 inflation expectations rising amid accommodative Fed policy
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with emerging inflation pressures
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (FEDFUNDS × CPIAUCSL) shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in housing starts (HOUST) above 1.7 million annualized units indicates strong residential construction demand and forward-looking consumer confidence, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as construction and materials stocks rally.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1700 (thousands of units) for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:May 2021 housing starts at 1739k, elevated post-pandemic
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong residential demand
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following HOUST > 1700 threshold are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) decelerates month-over-month while remaining above 105 index points, signaling supply-side constraints and pricing power that compress profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprrice earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2021 INDPRO at 106.31, post-pandemic supply chain stress
regime_hint: supply-constrained recovery with margin compression risk
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO momentum does not precede negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment (UNRATE) rises above 6.0% while employment growth (PAYEMS) simultaneously decelerates, the labor market is entering a deterioration phase that signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings pressure, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 6.0% AND PAYEMS month-over-month change < 100 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:May 2021 UNRATE at 6.1%, PAYEMS growth moderating
regime_hint: labor market deterioration phase with demand weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (UNRATE × PAYEMS momentum) shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 1.0% month-over-month, indicating robust consumer spending momentum that translates directly into corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2021 retail sales recovery from pandemic lows
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with strong consumer demand
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS acceleration does not correlate with positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized while the federal funds rate remains at zero lower bound, signaling stagnation risk that prompts the Fed to maintain or expand accommodation, supporting equity valuations through lower discount rates over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate < 2.0% AND FEDFUNDS < 0.15%
evidence_refs: fact:May 2021 GDPC1 at 19087.6B, post-pandemic recovery phase
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with growth deceleration risk
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 1.0% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) remains elevated above 5.5%, indicating supply-side recovery outpacing labor market normalization, which creates a favorable environment for productivity-driven earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2021 INDPRO at 106.31, UNRATE at 6.1%, supply recovery ahead of labor normalization
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (INDPRO × UNRATE) shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
